title: lerchenfeldia flexuosa subsp. montana
intro: Lerchenfeldia flexuosa subsp. montana is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the mountain Lerchenfeldia or mountain caterpillar's blood. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lerchenfeldia flexuosa subsp. montana include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create teas, or by drying the plant material to make infusions or tinctures.
